description:
`apps/funnel` is no longer a funnel. The signup → details → consent → payment funnel now lives in the marketing app (`apps/marketing/src/components/booking/`, built on `@pbh/booking`); what remains in `apps/funnel` is the authenticated post-purchase product — the assessments dashboard, report download, and login. The name now misleads anyone reading the tree.

Rename the directory and the workspace package to `app` (`apps/app`, package name `app`).

Known touch points (not exhaustive — grep `funnel` after moving):

Build / deploy config:
- `apps/funnel/package.json:2` — `"name": "funnel"`, plus the `dev`/`start` port flags
- `apps/funnel/vercel.json` — `"ignoreCommand": "npx turbo-ignore funnel"` must become the new package name, or preview builds will stop being skipped correctly
- root `tsconfig.json` — path references to `apps/funnel`
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` needs no change (globs `apps/*`), but `pnpm-lock.yaml` will need regenerating via `pnpm install`
- `.claude/settings.local.json` has funnel-scoped entries

Vercel:
- the Vercel project for this app has its **Root Directory** set to `apps/funnel` — that setting must be updated in the Vercel dashboard in the SAME change, or the deploy breaks. This is the step most likely to be forgotten.
- `pnpm --filter funnel …` appears in CI/docs/CLAUDE.md commands

Code + docs:
- `apps/funnel/src/app/robots.ts`, `next.config.ts`, `.env.example`, `src/app/page.tsx`
- `CLAUDE.md` "Workspace layout" section documents `apps/funnel/`
- ~17 files under `docs/` reference the funnel app, including `docs/sow2/technical/monorepo-plan.md`, `flow-diagrams.md`, and `diagrams/system-architecture.mmd`/`.svg`

Separately (decide whether to include or split out): the word "funnel" is used throughout `packages/booking/**` doc comments to mean "the non-marketing app" (e.g. `ConsentForm.tsx:45` "shared by the funnel and the marketing modal", `types.ts:4`, `webhook.ts:14`). Those comments become wrong once the funnel lives in marketing. Suggest fixing them in the same pass so the rename is not half-done.

Do this on a quiet branch — it touches nearly every workspace file and will conflict with anything in flight.
title: Rename the funnel app to "app" — it is not a funnel anymore

description:

Pull the full frame via Figma MCP (get_design_context on 751-2346) for exact spacing/inputs.

## Design (node 751-2346, "Extra Information" modal card, has an X close)
- Header: "Almost there!" + subcopy "Please, read carefully the following form to know what the terms of the assessments and consultation."
- Scrollable content viewport (content-viewport / scroll-content) with a custom scrollbar: an intro paragraph, several numbered legal sections (e.g. "1. Acceptance of Terms", "2. Use of Service", ...) each with a title + body, and an outro line.
- Checkbox: "I’ve read and agree to the consent form."
- CTA: "Continue With Payment".

## What to change
- Component: packages/booking/src/ConsentForm.tsx (rendered as the consent step by apps/marketing/src/components/booking/BookingStepFlow.tsx). It currently is basically a single agree checkbox -> advance; add the scrollable legal-terms panel + restyle header/checkbox/CTA.
- Keep the server contract: on agree, consent-core.ts (recordConsentCore) writes wellness + hipaa_npp rows at CONSENT_VERSION (packages/booking/src/server/consent.ts). The displayed terms should correspond to those consent types/version.
- Reuse @pbh/ui + design tokens; keep the checkbox -> CTA gating.

## SCOPE NOTE (2026-07-21)
This issue is the RESTYLE only. Sourcing and wiring the real consent copy moved
wholly to pbh-3v0 — the terms rendered here are still placeholder lorem, and that
is expected until pbh-3v0 lands. The former "OPEN QUESTION / do not ship lorem"
section and the "real terms, not lorem" acceptance criterion were removed here and
now live on pbh-3v0, so that issue is the single owner of the copy question
(constant vs Tina CMS vs legal-provided doc, plus the CONSENT_VERSION bump).

## Conventions
Token classes only, braces on every if, one component per file.

## Related
- pbh-3v0 (real wellness + HIPAA NPP copy) — owns the terms content.
- pbh-eaw (Figma token audit/reconciliation) — align spacing/colors/radii with tokens.
- pbh-bq8 (resume-at-step) — consent is a step; keep resume compatible.

## Acceptance criteria
- Consent step matches the Figma frame (header, scrollable terms panel w/ scrollbar, checkbox, "Continue With Payment" CTA).
- Agreeing still records wellness + hipaa_npp via consent-core.ts and advances to payment.
- Matches Figma in-browser (computed styles, not just classes); no raw hex.
- pnpm typecheck + lint changed files.

## Verification
pnpm --filter marketing dev (:3000); open the booking flow to the consent step, verify layout/scroll/gating, agree, and confirm it records consent + advances to payment.

## Deliberate divergence from Figma
The CTA stays ENABLED with an inline error on submit-without-agree, rather than the
Figma's disabled-until-checked state — per accessibility guidance from Cailin
Younger (Linus UX). A disabled button gives no feedback about why it can't be used.

Per Figma: PBH logo (left), a "Contact" link, and an authed Profile dropdown (avatar with user initial + "Profile" + caret). This differs from apps/marketing/src/components/layout/Header.tsx (which is marketing scroll-nav / IntersectionObserver / consultation CTA), so build a new funnel Header rather than reuse it.

## Footer (adapt marketing Footer)
Per Figma: brand block (logo + tagline), "Menu" column (About / What You Gain / How It Works / Trust), "Support" column (Contact / Terms & Conditions / Privacy Policy), copyright line, and contact@primarybrainhealth.com. This matches apps/marketing/src/components/layout/Footer.tsx closely.

## Integration

The centered min-h-screen page bodies (welcome/assessments) may need layout adjustment (e.g. flex column with header/main/footer) so the footer sits below content.

## Conventions
Braces on every if; one component per file (Header, Footer, and the Profile dropdown each in their own file); token classes only; "use client" only where interactivity (dropdown open/close) requires it.

## Related
Companion to pbh-chu (the "Choose How to Start" welcome screen, which was scoped to core content only and deferred this chrome). Not blocking — can be built in parallel.

## Acceptance criteria
- /welcome and /assessments render with the header (logo + Contact + Profile dropdown) and footer matching Figma.
- Profile dropdown opens/closes (keyboard + click-outside); items present even if stubbed.

description:
Write the agreed HIPAA session-timeout control set into `docs/sow2/technical/auth-plan.md` so it survives the auth-provider decision. This is the record of the requirement; it does not depend on which provider wins.

Background (Stefanie, Slack): HIPAA does not prescribe a specific session-timeout duration — it requires an automatic logoff control based on the organization's risk assessment. Because the authenticated dashboard provides access to the Linus report, the dashboard session becomes part of the protected access pathway even though the report itself stays stored in Linus.

Controls to document:
- 15-minute inactivity timeout on the authenticated dashboard
- 8-hour absolute session cap, so an active session cannot remain authenticated indefinitely
- single-use sign-in link with a 10–15 minute TTL (applies if magic links are chosen)
- scope: the ENTIRE authenticated dashboard, not only the report page

Note: the doc's current "Session strategy" section specifies a 30-day sliding expiration. That must be replaced, not appended to.

Deliberately NOT included: a step-up re-authentication gate before opening or downloading the report. Stefanie raised it in the original thread, but it was dropped from scope — the idle + absolute timeouts are the control set we are committing to. Revisit only if compliance asks for it again.
title: Document HIPAA session-timeout controls in auth-plan.md

description:
`docs/sow2/technical/auth-plan.md` recommends Auth.js v5 with a credentials (password) provider. The compliance thread assumes magic links ("I kinda hate this with a magic link, but it's the easiest right now" — Stefanie). No magic-link implementation exists today: `apps/funnel/src/app/login/LoginForm.tsx` is a placeholder that accepts any email and drops an unsigned user-id cookie with no verification step at all.

These are different builds with different timeout semantics — a password flow has no link TTL; a magic-link flow needs single-use tokens, a verification-token table, and Resend wiring.

Decide and record the outcome in `auth-plan.md`. Inputs: the doc's own Clerk / Auth.js / custom-JWT comparison (Clerk needs Enterprise for a BAA), the Resend transactional-email integration already scoped in pbh-bws.27, and the `verification_token` table the doc already sketches, which a magic-link flow would reuse.

Blocks the session-timeout enforcement work.
title: Decide the funnel auth provider: magic link vs password

description:
Implement the automatic-logoff control across every authenticated funnel surface — not just the report page.

Today the only session control is `ASSESSMENT_COOKIE_OPTS` in `apps/funnel/src/app/assessments/actions.ts:24`: an unsigned user id, `maxAge: 60 * 60`, no idle tracking, no absolute cap, no server-side revocation. Every authenticated surface reads the cookie directly:
- `apps/funnel/src/app/assessments/page.tsx:22` (dashboard)
- `apps/funnel/src/app/assessments/actions.ts:84` (`getReportPdf`)

Deliver, on top of whatever provider the auth decision picks:
- a signed or opaque server-side session rather than a raw uid cookie
- last-activity tracking with a 15-minute idle expiry
- an absolute 8-hour cap independent of activity
- ONE shared session-read helper so every authenticated route enforces it, rather than per-route cookie reads. `resolveBookingUserId` in `packages/booking/src/server/auth.ts` is the existing precedent for a single identity choke point — follow that shape.
- an audit-log event on session expiry / logout. `AuditEventType` in `packages/db/src/audit.ts:9` will need a new member.

Note: `ASSESSMENT_UID_COOKIE` is defined in `packages/booking/src/server/register-and-enroll.ts:25` and is also the handoff mechanism from the marketing payment step, so changing its shape touches both apps.
title: Enforce 15-min idle + 8-hr absolute session timeout across authenticated funnel routes

description:
After the user submits their email in the marketing booking flow, resume them at the correct point. NEW users go straight into the modal at the first step; RETURNING (known) users see a "Welcome back" screen and must confirm via an emailed link before resuming at their first incomplete step. If everything is already complete, hand off to the funnel.

Figma:
- Welcome-back screen ("Already in database"): https://www.figma.com/design/SppKdzsaH6rQ14u90UpNSq/Primary-Brain-Health?node-id=896-1479&m=dev

## Flow
1. Booking form email submit -> resolveBookingProgress(email) looks up the user.
2. NEW email -> create user (createAccountCore) -> open modal at "details" (no confirmation needed; nothing to protect).
3. RETURNING email (user exists, not fully complete) -> show the "Welcome back, {firstName}" modal (Figma 896-1479) and SEND a confirmation email. Do NOT open the step modal yet.
4. User clicks the emailed magic link -> verify token -> resume the booking modal at the FIRST INCOMPLETE step (details -> consent -> payment) computed by resolveBookingProgress.
5. Already fully complete (details + consent + succeeded payment) -> hand off to funnel /login?email= (same as DoneStep) -> /assessments.

## Welcome back screen (Figma 896-1479, "Extra Information" card)
- Title: "Welcome back, {firstName}"
- Body: "You already started the process previously. We’ve sent you an email. Please check your inbox, and confirm this is you."
- CTA: "Continue Where I left" (re-send/return affordance; the actual resume happens via the emailed link).
- Rendered as a modal/card over the booking section; reuse the existing Modal shell (apps/marketing/src/components/booking/Modal.tsx) + @pbh/ui + tokens.

## Current behavior (to change)
- BookingStepFlow.tsx uses a useState stepIndex over MODAL_STEPS = ["details","consent","payment","done"]; start() HARDCODES setStepIndex(0). advance() uses Math.min, so starting mid-sequence already works.
- Email is collected by inline SignupForm (first/last/email) -> signupAction (apps/marketing/src/components/booking/actions.ts) -> createAccountCore (packages/booking/src/server/signup-core.ts), which INSERTs a users row and REJECTS duplicate emails ("account already exists"). Must change to lookup-or-create.

## Completeness signals (all NEW reads; consents/payments are write-only today)
- details incomplete if users.dateOfBirth is null (also stateOfResidence, phone, gender, educationLevel, patientIdentification; message optional). packages/db/src/schema/users.ts; set by details-core.ts.
- consent incomplete if no consents row for user_id. packages/db/src/schema/consents.ts.
- payment incomplete if no payments row with status="succeeded" for user_id. packages/db/src/schema/payments.ts (fulfill.ts recordSucceededPayment). A bare row is NOT sufficient (failed/refunded can exist).

## Implementation
1. resolveBookingProgress core in packages/booking/src/server/ (e.g. booking-progress.ts): normalize email; SELECT users WHERE email = ? (pattern in register-and-enroll.ts:499-519). No user -> createAccountCore -> step "details" (new-user path). User exists -> first incomplete step, or "complete". Returns { userId, email, firstName, step:"details"|"consent"|"payment"|"complete", isReturning:boolean }.
2. Returning + not complete -> render Welcome back modal + trigger sendResumeConfirmationEmail(userId). Wire signupAction accordingly.
3. Extend SignupState/SignupResult (packages/booking/src/types.ts) to carry step + isReturning (+ complete).
4. BookingStepFlow.start(): for the new-user path, setStepIndex(Math.max(0, MODAL_STEPS.indexOf(step))); for returning, show Welcome back modal instead; if step==="complete", navigate to ${NEXT_PUBLIC_FUNNEL_URL}/login?email=... (reuse DoneStep handoff).
5. Magic-link resume: verify route validates the token and opens the booking modal at the resolved first-incomplete step.

## Email confirmation infrastructure (NEW PREREQUISITE - none exists today)
There is NO email-sending setup in the repo (no resend/nodemailer/sendgrid/ses, no token/verify code). This feature needs:
- An email-sending capability + provider (recommend Resend; works cleanly on Vercel) + a confirmation email template.
- A confirmation token: signed (HMAC/JWT, short TTL) OR a DB table (e.g. booking_resume_tokens with expiry) tied to userId + intended step; single-use.
- A verify/resume route that validates the token and enters the resumed flow.
This is sizable and separable; consider splitting it into its own prerequisite sub-task that this one depends on.

## Security / privacy
The email confirmation is the mitigation for unauthenticated resume + PII/enumeration: DO NOT open the step modal or reveal stored data on bare email entry. To avoid account enumeration, show the same "we’ve sent you an email" outcome whether or not the email exists (only send the email when it does). Tokens must be single-use + short-lived. Revisit with real auth (resolveBookingUserId is a TODO(clerk)).

## Acceptance criteria
- New email -> modal opens at details (no email confirmation).
- Returning email (partial) -> Welcome back modal shown + confirmation email sent; step modal NOT opened until the emailed link is clicked; link resumes at the correct first incomplete step (consent if details done; payment if details+consent done).
- Fully-onboarded/paid email -> handed off to funnel /login -> /assessments.
- Bare email entry never reveals whether an account exists nor exposes stored PII; tokens single-use + expiring.
- Payment completeness uses status="succeeded", not just a row.

## Verification
Seed users at each state (new/partial/complete) in a test DB. pnpm --filter marketing dev (:3000); submit each email and confirm: new -> details; returning -> Welcome back + email; click link -> resumes at expected step; complete -> funnel handoff. pnpm typecheck + lint.

Refresh/resume gap (follow-up, tied to auth — pbh-bws.18/FT18 Clerk session):

Consent step shipped on branch tuily/pbh-bws.4 (commit e5341f8) as a single-page client stepper (StepFlow.tsx). Step position + userId live in CLIENT MEMORY only. Proper fix rides with auth/session: on /get-started, derive the resume step server-side from existing state (account exists? consent rows exist? payment?) instead of client memory. - First-login state -> new nullable users.welcomeSeenAt timestamptz (Clerk is NOT integrated, so DB is the only store).

- Start With Navigator -> placeholder alert() referencing the companion task; wired up separately.

## Implementation
1. DB: add welcomeSeenAt: timestamp("welcome_seen_at", { withTimezone: true }) (nullable) to packages/db/src/schema/users.ts (match linusRegistrationClaimedAt style). Run pnpm --filter @pbh/db run db:generate -> commit new packages/db/src/migrations/0008_*.sql. Migrations apply on PROD deploy only (apps/funnel/vercel.json); for local/preview apply the column to the dev DB via the db-migrate skill.
2. Gate in one place: in apps/funnel/src/app/assessments/actions.ts (registerAndEnroll, ~line 57) change post-login redirect from /assessments to /welcome.
3. New route apps/funnel/src/app/welcome/ (mirror assessments/ conventions):
   - page.tsx: async server component + guard. Read ASSESSMENT_UID_COOKIE; missing -> redirect("/login"). Load user; if welcomeSeenAt set -> redirect("/assessments"). Else render centered <main flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center> + Container size="narrow" + Heading + subcopy + two Cards, reusing @pbh/ui (Button, Container, Heading, Card).
   - WelcomeActions.tsx: "use client" sibling with the two CTAs. Start With Navigator -> Button onClick alert placeholder. Start Assessments -> <form action={markWelcomeSeen}> submit Button.
   - actions.ts: "use server" markWelcomeSeen(): resolve uid from ASSESSMENT_UID_COOKIE, db.update(users).set({ welcomeSeenAt: sql`now()` }).where(eq(users.id, uid)).returning({ id: users.id }) (mirror packages/booking/src/server/details-core.ts:102-115), then redirect("/assessments").

## Figma copy (verbatim)
- Headline: Choose How to Start
- Subcopy: You've already made an important decision for your brain health; now you can choose how you'd like to begin your care journey. Both options lead to a navigator and a clear plan - this just helps you pick the path that fits you best.
- Card 1: title "Talk to an Expert" / body "For individuals and care partners who feel overwhelmed, have many questions, or want guidance before diving into testing." / CTA "Start With Navigator"
- Card 2: title "Start with Assessments" / body "Best for individuals and care partners who are ready to move forward now and want results to guide the conversation." / CTA "Start Assessments"

Braces on every if; one component per file; token classes only (no raw hex).

## Acceptance criteria
- Fresh user (welcomeSeenAt null) lands on /welcome after login; sees the two-card screen.
- Start With Navigator shows placeholder alert; does not navigate.
- Start Assessments sets welcomeSeenAt and redirects to /assessments.

- Unauthenticated hit to /welcome redirects to /login.

description:
Stand up Playwright end-to-end testing covering the whole onboarding flow across both apps: marketing booking (:3000) through the funnel handoff (:3001).

## Current state
- No E2E Playwright setup. "playwright" is present only transitively via @vitest/browser-playwright (Storybook vitest browser mode in apps/marketing) — there is NO @playwright/test, no playwright.config, no e2e specs, no root "test" script, and no CI (.github does not exist yet).
- Monorepo: pnpm@10.27 + Turborepo. Apps: marketing (:3000, tinacms dev), funnel (:3001).

## Setup
1. Add @playwright/test as a dev dependency and run npx playwright install (chromium at minimum).
2. Create an e2e location (e.g. top-level e2e/ or tests/e2e/) with playwright.config.ts. Use two webServer entries to boot marketing (:3000) and funnel (:3001) — or run against a shared "pnpm dev" — with baseURL per app / project.
3. Add a root "test:e2e" script and a Turborepo task (mark it non-cached / no outputs).
4. Add stable selectors (data-testid) to the booking form fields + step CTAs and the funnel pages as needed for reliable targeting.

## Happy-path spec (the "whole flow")
Marketing: land on the booking section -> fill signup (firstName, lastName, email) -> modal opens -> details step (DOB, ZIP, state, phone, gender, education, patient id) -> consent step (agree) -> payment step (Stripe Embedded Checkout, test card 4242 4242 4242 4242) -> done step -> click continue -> funnel /login?email= -> /assessments renders "Welcome Back, {firstName}".

Component/flow references: apps/marketing/src/components/booking/ (BookingStepFlow, Modal, DoneStep), packages/booking/src/ (SignupForm, DetailsForm, ConsentForm, PaymentStep), funnel apps/funnel/src/app/login + /assessments.

## Environment / infrastructure decisions & known hurdles (call out in implementation)
- Test DB: run against a DEDICATED test DB / Neon branch, NOT prod. Note: Vercel previews currently share the prod Neon DB, so do not point E2E at a preview URL that writes prod. Need per-run seeding + cleanup (users/consents/payments rows).
- Stripe: use TEST keys and an ACTIVE sandbox price (an archived/inactive price makes "start payment" fail — this bit us before). Decide how fulfillment is confirmed in tests: the onComplete verify path vs the webhook (STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET + stripe listen/forwarding). Interacting with the Stripe Checkout iframe for card entry needs frameLocator handling.
- Linus: the Linus sandbox API is US-only — enrollment 403s (surfacing as a webhook/enroll failure) unless the runner is on a US IP/VPN. For CI this likely requires stubbing/mocking Linus or a US-region runner; otherwise the enroll step of the full flow will fail.
- Secrets: Stripe test keys, DATABASE_URL (test branch), LINUS_* sandbox, NEXT_PUBLIC_FUNNEL_URL must be provided to the test env.

## CI (optional / follow-up)
No CI exists. Consider adding a GitHub Actions workflow to run the E2E suite on PRs (with the secrets above and playwright browsers cached). Can be split into its own task.

## Follow-up coverage
Once built, extend the suite to cover the new first-login welcome screen (pbh-chu) and the resume-at-first-incomplete-step booking behavior (pbh-bq8).

## Acceptance criteria
- @playwright/test installed; playwright.config.ts boots/targets both apps; "pnpm test:e2e" (or turbo run test:e2e) executes the suite locally.
- A green happy-path E2E drives marketing booking -> Stripe test payment -> funnel handoff -> /assessments.
- Test data is isolated (test DB/branch) and cleaned up between runs; docs/README note the required env + how to run.
title: Set up Playwright E2E to test the full onboarding flow (marketing -> funnel)

## Design (node 751-2529, "Extra Information" modal card, has an X close)
- Header: "Payment" + subcopy "This service may be eligible for HSA/FSA reimbursement, depending on your plan. We can provide documentation to support submission."
- Checkout card: line item "Brain health assessment" + price "$149.00"; Email (prefilled); Payment method (card number, MM / YY, CVC + help, Cardholder name, Country or region = United States + ZIP); "Save my information for faster checkout" / "Pay securely on future visits" toggle; phone field with country flag.
- CTA: "Pay $149.00".

## KEY DECISION / RISK (resolve before implementing)
The current payment step uses Stripe EMBEDDED CHECKOUT — checkout.sessions with ui_mode="embedded_page" (packages/booking/src/server/checkout-core.ts, rendered by packages/booking/src/PaymentStep.tsx via payment/actions.ts createAssessmentCheckoutSession). That renders Stripe hosted UI in an iframe with limited layout control. The Figma shows a fully app-styled form. Achieving it likely means EITHER:
  (a) customize the Stripe Checkout appearance API (fonts/colors/radii to match @pbh/tokens) within Embedded Checkout layout constraints, OR
  (b) migrate to the Stripe Payment Element (PaymentIntent) for full layout/style control.
Decide and note the tradeoffs. Whichever path: the amount/currency/product name MUST still come from the Stripe catalog price (source of truth — reuse getAssessmentCatalogEntry; do NOT hardcode $149), and fulfillment (verifyAndRecordCheckout + the webhook backstop, finalizeCheckoutSession) must keep working.

## What to change
- packages/booking/src/PaymentStep.tsx (+ possibly checkout-core.ts / payment/actions.ts depending on path chosen).
- App-rendered chrome (header, "$X" summary, "Pay $X" CTA) uses @pbh/ui + tokens; the $ amount is dynamic from the catalog.

## Conventions
Token classes only, braces on every if, one component per file.

## Related
- pbh-eaw (Figma token audit) — align appearance with tokens.
- pbh-bq8 (resume-at-step) — payment is a step; keep resume compatible.
- Note prior sandbox findings: needs Stripe test keys + an ACTIVE price; restricted-key scopes must allow PaymentIntents/Checkout writes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Payment step matches the Figma frame (header + subcopy, checkout card w/ summary/email/card fields/save-info/phone, "Pay $X" CTA).
- Price/label come from the Stripe catalog (not hardcoded); payment completes with a test card and fulfillment still records + enrolls.
- Chosen Stripe approach (appearance API vs Payment Element) documented in the PR.
- Matches Figma in-browser (computed styles); no raw hex in app-rendered chrome.
- pnpm typecheck + lint changed files.

## Verification
pnpm --filter marketing dev (:3000) with Stripe test keys + active price; open booking to the payment step, compare to Figma, pay with 4242 4242 4242 4242, and confirm fulfillment (payment recorded, user enrolled/handed off).
title: Update the payment form to match the Figma design

## Part 2 — Reconcile (apply fixes)
- Tokens (packages/tokens/theme.css): add missing tokens Figma defines — a radius token (button/radius 40 -> e.g. --radius-pill), spacing tokens for recurring nav/button/footer/faq gaps + paddings, missing colors (#EFF6F9 as a surface token; reconcile #6f706e vs on-surface-variant #44474d). Re-expose new tokens in @theme inline; also expose :root tokens missing from @theme inline (e.g. --color-primary-fixed) if used.
- Raw-hex migration (~5 live usages -> token classes): apps/marketing/src/app/layout.tsx:72 (bg-[#EFF6F9]); apps/marketing/src/components/blocks/StackSections/StackSections.tsx:230 (#EFF6F9 + arbitrary shadow); apps/marketing/src/components/blocks/BenefitsList/BenefitsList.tsx:36 (#E2EFEF); apps/marketing/src/components/layout/Footer.tsx:7 (#EFF6F9); apps/funnel/src/app/assessments/AssessmentCard.tsx:30 (border-[#d8d8d8]).
- Fonts: standardize headline on Larken (align theme.css "Larken" with the Typekit "larken" loaded in apps/marketing/src/app/layout.tsx + apps/funnel/src/app/layout.tsx); define --font-inter consistently in funnel (or load Inter via next/font there); remove dead Gilda_Display wiring in marketing; update CLAUDE.md to drop the stale "Manrope" reference (real headline font is Larken).

## Out of scope (possible follow-up)
Standing enforcement: stylelint/eslint no-hex rule, Figma Code Connect, visual-regression snapshots (could fold into pbh-d5a).

## Acceptance criteria
- docs/design-audit.md lists every Figma-vs-code discrepancy for in-scope screens (Figma value vs code value + severity).
- New spacing/radius/color tokens added + exposed to Tailwind; the ~5 raw-hex usages migrated (grep #[0-9a-fA-F]{6} in apps/** + packages/**/src shows no live off-token color).
- Font wiring consistent across both apps (headline = Larken; funnel body Inter resolves); CLAUDE.md updated.
- In-browser render of each in-scope screen visually matches the Figma frame.

## Verification
